title: lauridia tetragona
intro: Lauridia tetragona, also known as the forest marigold, is a flowering plant native to the eastern parts of southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Lauridia tetragona include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
